title: CI Switch: Cirrus -> TMT
body:
I'd like to switch CI from Cirrus to TMT and get rid of Cirrus (as much as possible if not fully). Filing this to determine the work that needs to be done toward this:

Current list of tests in Cirrus (along with TMT status)

**In TMT (main and/or needs forward porting):**
- system (main)
- integration (needs forward porting)
- validate (needs forward porting)
- unit (needs forward porting)

**Not yet in TMT:**
- doccheck / validate-docs (doable)
- cross (doable)
- osx (unlikely in the near future, OSX not available via TMT)
- ostree-rs-ext (still needed?)
- w/ opengpg (still needed?)

**Additional requirements for TMT:**
- Stable testing images for no surprises like dependency breakage (hopefully not that big an issue for skopeo,  I'll check further with TMT team)

comments.body:
> ostree-rs-ext (still needed?)

We do have some tests of the proxy code in `integration/proxy_test.go`, but an integration test against the primary user still seems valuable. OTOH _maybe_ that would be better modeled as a RPM reverse dependency test? I have no idea. (Cc: @cgwalters )

> w/ opengpg (still needed?)

Some users vehemently ask a dependency-free (probably Go-only) stand-alone binary that can be run in any environment; they probably need that build. So, I think we should be testing that. I can imagine downgrading this to a compile test + a minimal smoke-test, but with my upstream hat on, we should have _some_ tests. (Compare https://github.com/containers/image/issues/2877 , build tags which we don’t test have a tendency to rot.)

> ostree-rs-ext (still needed?)

In theory with testing-farm AIUI there's some support for reverse dependency testing, so triggering the bootc tests on changes to skopeo both upstream and downstream should be doable.

> > ostree-rs-ext (still needed?)
> 
> In theory with testing-farm AIUI there's some support for reverse dependency testing, so triggering the bootc tests on changes to skopeo both upstream and downstream should be doable.

Yup, in progress at https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2640 . Thanks.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
